    return '01234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789'

x = string.dump(load("x = 1; return x"))
a = assert(load(read1(x), nil, "b"))

cannotload("attempt to load a binary chunk", load(read1(x), nil, "t"))
cannotload("attempt to load a binary chunk", load(x, nil, "t"))
_G.x = nil

assert(not pcall(string.dump, print))  -- no dump of C functions

cannotload("unexpected symbol", load(read1("*a = 123")))
cannotload("unexpected symbol", load("*a = 123"))
cannotload("hhi", load(function () error("hhi") end))

-- any value is valid for _ENV
assert(load("return _ENV", nil, nil, 123)() == 123)

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi

490	///
491	/// The `writer` callback receives successive slices of the serialised bytes and returns
492	/// `Err(LuaError)` to abort. `strip` omits debug info (line numbers, local names, etc.)
493	/// from the output.
494	///
495	/// C: `int luaU_dump(lua_State *L, const Proto *f, lua_Writer w, void *data, int strip)`
496	///
497	/// PORT NOTE: `lua_Writer w` (fn pointer) + `void *data` (userdata) are collapsed into a
498	/// single `impl FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError>` closure — the Rust idiom for the
499	/// callback + context pair. `_state` is retained in the signature for API parity but unused
500	/// in the body: the C code needed it only for `lua_lock`/`lua_unlock`, which are no-ops per
501	/// macros.tsv. Return type changes from `int` (0 = ok, non-zero = writer error) to
502	/// `Result<(), LuaError>`.
503	pub(crate) fn dump(
504	    _state: &mut LuaState,
505	    proto: &GcRef<LuaProto>,
506	    writer: impl FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError> + 'static,
507	    strip: bool,
508	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
509	    // C: DumpState D; D.L = L; D.writer = w; D.data = data; D.strip = strip; D.status = 0;
510	    let mut d = DumpState {
511	        writer: Box::new(writer),
512	        strip,
513	    };
514	
515	    // C: dumpHeader(&D);
516	    d.dump_header()?;
517	
518	    // C: dumpByte(&D, f->sizeupvalues);
519	    // PORT NOTE: f->sizeupvalues is covered by Vec::len(). Bounded by MAXUPVAL = 255
520	    // (macros.tsv), so truncation via `as u8` is safe for well-formed prototypes.
521	    d.dump_byte(proto.upvalues.len() as u8)?;
522	
523	    // C: dumpFunction(&D, f, NULL);
524	    // psource = None forces the top-level function to always write its source name.
525	    // Deref coercion: &GcRef<LuaProto> → &LuaProto (via Deref<Target=LuaProto> on GcRef/Rc).
526	    d.dump_function(proto, None)?;
527	
528	    // C: return D.status;
529	    Ok(())
530	}
531

1	//! Pre-compiled Lua chunk serializer.
2	//!
3	//! Translates `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/ldump.c` (230 lines, 9 functions + 1 public entry point).
4	//! Writes a `LuaProto` to a byte sink in the standard Lua 5.4 bytecode format.
5	
6	// C: #define ldump_c / #define LUA_CORE — build guards; not needed in Rust.
7	
8	// TODO(port): Adjust import paths once crate boundaries stabilise in Phase B.
9	// The types below are expected to resolve as follows:
10	//   GcRef        — lua_types (or lua-gc Phase D)
11	//   LuaError     — lua_types

delta.text: The
reproducer now prints `function` and `1` as expected. No instrumentation was

added so nothing to remove. Build is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** Two bugs in `string.dump`:
1. `crates/lua-st
dlib/src/string_lib.rs:379` — `str_dump` called `state.set_top(1)` (the inherent `LuaState` method, which takes an *
absolute* `StackIdx`). This clobbered the call frame so that the next `state.get_at(top - 1)` read
slot 0 (nil) instead of the user's function. The same PORT NOTE war

ning already exists at `string_lib.rs:421` for `trymt`.
2. `crates/lua-vm/src
/api.rs:1771` — `api::dump` delegated to `state.dump_proto(...)`, a Phase-B
`todo!()` stub at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1938`. With bug #1 also making the `if let Lua

Closure::Lua` branch never match, the stub never panicked, so `string.dump` silently returned an empty string.

**Fix:**
-

Changed `str_dump` to call frame-relative `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, 1)?`.
- Rewired `api::dump` to call the real
`crate::dump::dump` implementation that already exists in `crates/lua-vm/src/dump.rs:503`. Relaxed `
crate::dump::dump`'s writer parameter from `impl FnMut + 'static` to `&mut dyn FnMut
` (and added an `'a` lifetime on `DumpState`) so callers can pass a closure borrowing a

local `Vec<u8>` buffer.

**New state:** `f = load(string.dump(function () return 1 end), nil, "b", {})` now produ
ces `f() == 1`. The minimal reproducer for `testes/calls
.lua:332` passes; the full file was not re-run (the mega-loop will re-test).

content.text:
The reproducer now prints `function` and `1` as expected. No instrumentation was added so nothing to remove. Build is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** Two bugs in `string.dump`:
1. `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:379` — `str_dump` called `state.set_top(1)` (the inherent `LuaState` method, which takes an *absolute* `StackIdx`). This clobbered the call frame so that the next `state.get_at(top - 1)` read slot 0 (nil) instead of the user's function. The same PORT NOTE warning already exists at `string_lib.rs:421` for `trymt`.
2. `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1771` — `api::dump` delegated to `state.dump_proto(...)`, a Phase-B `todo!()` stub at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1938`. With bug #1 also making the `if let LuaClosure::Lua` branch never match, the stub never panicked, so `string.dump` silently returned an empty string.

**Fix:**
- Changed `str_dump` to call frame-relative `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, 1)?`.
- Rewired `api::dump` to call the real `crate::dump::dump` implementation that already exists in `crates/lua-vm/src/dump.rs:503`. Relaxed `crate::dump::dump`'s writer parameter from `impl FnMut + 'static` to `&mut dyn FnMut` (and added an `'a` lifetime on `DumpState`) so callers can pass a closure borrowing a local `Vec<u8>` buffer.

**New state:** `f = load(string.dump(function () return 1 end), nil, "b", {})` now produces `f() == 1`. The minimal reproducer for `testes/calls.lua:332` passes; the full file was not re-run (the mega-loop will re-test).

result:
The reproducer now prints `function` and `1` as expected. No instrumentation was added so nothing to remove. Build is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** Two bugs in `string.dump`:
1. `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:379` — `str_dump` called `state.set_top(1)` (the inherent `LuaState` method, which takes an *absolute* `StackIdx`). This clobbered the call frame so that the next `state.get_at(top - 1)` read slot 0 (nil) instead of the user's function. The same PORT NOTE warning already exists at `string_lib.rs:421` for `trymt`.
2. `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1771` — `api::dump` delegated to `state.dump_proto(...)`, a Phase-B `todo!()` stub at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1938`. With bug #1 also making the `if let LuaClosure::Lua` branch never match, the stub never panicked, so `string.dump` silently returned an empty string.

**Fix:**
- Changed `str_dump` to call frame-relative `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, 1)?`.
- Rewired `api::dump` to call the real `crate::dump::dump` implementation that already exists in `crates/lua-vm/src/dump.rs:503`. Relaxed `crate::dump::dump`'s writer parameter from `impl FnMut + 'static` to `&mut dyn FnMut` (and added an `'a` lifetime on `DumpState`) so callers can pass a closure borrowing a local `Vec<u8>` buffer.

**New state:** `f = load(string.dump(function () return 1 end), nil, "b", {})` now produces `f() == 1`. The minimal reproducer for `testes/calls.lua:332` passes; the full file was not re-run (the mega-loop will re-test).
